title: pallavicinia fragrans
intro: Pallavicinia fragrans, also known as the fragrant liverwort, is a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Pallaviciniaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pallavicinia fragrans include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion. Herbal preparations of Pallavicinia fragrans typically involve drying the plant and using it in the form of teas, infusions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
